topic: Someone discovers their recipe has become famous under another name
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elara stared at the glossy magazine spread, the headline screaming the name 'The Golden Whisk.' Her hands, usually steady when kneading dough, were trembling slightly as she traced the elegant script. It was her recipe—a simple sourdough starter elevated by a secret blend of wild yeast and ancient grain—that was now the subject of international culinary acclaim. She felt a strange, hollow ache settle in her chest, a sudden lightness followed by a profound emptiness. She picked up her phone, her thumb hovering over the contact for her old baking friend, Maya. A slow, unsteady breath escaped her lips. How could this be? She reread the article, the words blurring into an unfamiliar landscape.

[story 2]

"This is impossible," Marcus muttered, running a hand through his already disheveled hair. He leaned against the counter, the cool granite offering little comfort against the heat rising in his cheeks. The online reviews were unanimous; the "Midnight Loaf" was the sensation, the undisputed champion of artisanal bread. He looked down at the handwritten card tucked beneath his cutting board—his own scribbled notes from a decade ago. A slow, deliberate shake of his head preceded a sharp intake of breath. He walked to the window, peering out at the bustling city street, feeling suddenly small and exposed under the glare of the afternoon sun. The reality of the situation settled over him like a heavy blanket.

[story 3]

The notification popped up on her tablet, a cascade of praise for 'The Sun-Kissed Crumb.' Lena blinked, her gaze fixed on the screen, unable to process the influx of accolades. She dropped the ceramic mug she was holding; it hit the tile floor with a dull thud. A wave of disorientation washed over her, making the edges of the room swim. She began pacing the small kitchen, her movements jerky and uncoordinated. "No," she whispered, the sound barely audible above the hum of the refrigerator. She stopped abruptly, pressing her palms against her eyes, trying to force the dazzling images on the screen to recede, but they only sharpened, highlighting the strange, unsettling truth of the situation.
